translations.descricao: Psoriasis is a chronic inflammatory and immune-mediated skin disease, characterized by erythematosquamous plaques. Global prevalence varies from 1% to 3%. In Brazil, it is estimated at 1% to 2% of the population. The current Brazilian consensus updates management, including treatment algorithms. Biosimilars are recommended to reduce costs in biological therapies.
translations.sus.indicacao: Clinical diagnosis for skin lesions suspected of psoriasis; accessible treatment via SUS for moderate to severe cases.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults and children with persistent skin symptoms or family history.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The Brazilian Society of Dermatology recommends diagnosis based on clinical criteria and stepped treatment by severity. Portuguese position endorses biosimilars for moderate to severe psoriasis.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Annual incidence of 0.1-0.2% in Caucasian populations; lower in Latin populations.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Low directly, but increases risk of cardiovascular comorbidities (RR 1.5-2.0).
